name: netexec
title: netexec

commands.name: netexec

arguments.description: show this help message and exit
arguments.raw: -h, --help            show this help message and exit

arguments.description: Display nxc version
max timeout in seconds of each thread
arguments.raw: --timeout TIMEOUT     max timeout in seconds of each thread

arguments.description: sets a random delay between each authentication
arguments.raw: --jitter INTERVAL     sets a random delay between each authentication

arguments.description: do not displaying progress bar during scan
arguments.raw: --no-progress         do not displaying progress bar during scan

arguments.description: export result into a custom file
arguments.raw: --log LOG             export result into a custom file

arguments.description: enable verbose output
enable debug level information
Enable force IPv6
Use TCP instead of UDP for DNS queries
arguments.raw: --dns-tcp             Use TCP instead of UDP for DNS queries

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# netexec -h
 usage: netexec [-h] [--version] [-t THREADS] [--timeout TIMEOUT]
              [--jitter INTERVAL] [--no-progress] [--log LOG] [--verbose |
              --debug] [-6] [--dns-server DNS_SERVER] [--dns-tcp]
              [--dns-timeout DNS_TIMEOUT]
              {wmi,smb,winrm,mssql,ftp,vnc,ssh,nfs,rdp,ldap} ...
 
    .   .
   .|   |.     _   _          _     _____
   ||   ||    | \ | |   ___  | |_  | ____| __  __   ___    ___
   \\( )//    |  \| |  / _ \ | __| |  _|   \ \/ /  / _ \  / __|
   .=[ ]=.    | |\  | |  __/ | |_  | |___   >  <  |  __/ | (__
  / /˙-˙\ \   |_| \_|  \___|  \__| |_____| /_/\_\  \___|  \___|
  ˙ \   / ˙
    ˙   ˙
 
   The network execution tool
   Maintained as an open source project by @NeffIsBack, @MJHallenbeck, @_zblurx
 
   For documentation and usage examples, visit: https://www.netexec.wiki/
 
   Version : 1.5.1
   Codename: Yippie-Ki-Yay
   Commit  : Kali Linux
   
 
 options:
 -h, --help            show this help message and exit
 
 Generic Options:
 --version             Display nxc version
 -t, --threads THREADS
                       set how many concurrent threads to use
 --timeout TIMEOUT     max timeout in seconds of each thread
 --jitter INTERVAL     sets a random delay between each authentication
 
 Output Options:
 --no-progress         do not displaying progress bar during scan
 --log LOG             export result into a custom file
 --verbose             enable verbose output
 --debug               enable debug level information
 
 DNS:
 -6                    Enable force IPv6
 --dns-server DNS_SERVER
                       Specify DNS server (default: Use hosts file & System DNS)
 --dns-tcp             Use TCP instead of UDP for DNS queries
 --dns-timeout DNS_TIMEOUT
                       DNS query timeout in seconds
 
 Available Protocols:
 {wmi,smb,winrm,mssql,ftp,vnc,ssh,nfs,rdp,ldap}
   wmi                 own stuff using WMI
   smb                 own stuff using SMB
   winrm               own stuff using WINRM
   mssql               own stuff using MSSQL
   ftp                 own stuff using FTP
   vnc                 own stuff using VNC
   ssh                 own stuff using SSH
   nfs                 own stuff using NFS
   rdp                 own stuff using RDP
   ldap                own stuff using LDAP

examples.command: netexec: error: argument protocol: invalid choice: 'help' (choose from 'rdp', 'mssql', 'ftp', 'nfs', 'smb', 'vnc', 'ldap', 'wmi', 'ssh', 'winrm')

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit

Generic Options:
  --version             Display nxc version
  -t, --threads THREADS
                        set how many concurrent threads to use
  --timeout TIMEOUT     max timeout in seconds of each thread
  --jitter INTERVAL     sets a random delay between each authentication

Output Options:
  --no-progress         do not displaying progress bar during scan
  --log LOG             export result into a custom file
  --verbose             enable verbose output
  --debug               enable debug level information

DNS:
  -6                    Enable force IPv6
  --dns-server DNS_SERVER
                        Specify DNS server (default: Use hosts file & System DNS)
  --dns-tcp             Use TCP instead of UDP for DNS queries
  --dns-timeout DNS_TIMEOUT
                        DNS query timeout in seconds

Available Protocols:
  {rdp,mssql,ftp,nfs,smb,vnc,ldap,wmi,ssh,winrm}
    rdp                 own stuff using RDP
    mssql               own stuff using MSSQL
    ftp                 own stuff using FTP
    nfs                 own stuff using NFS
    smb                 own stuff using SMB
    vnc                 own stuff using VNC
    ldap                own stuff using LDAP
    wmi                 own stuff using WMI
    ssh                 own stuff using SSH
    winrm               own stuff using WINRM

commands.name: nxcdb

arguments.description: show this help message and exit
arguments.raw: -h, --help            show this help message and exit

arguments.description: get the current workspace

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# nxcdb -h
 usage: nxcdb [-h] [-gw] [-cw CREATE_WORKSPACE] [-sw SET_WORKSPACE]
 
 NXCDB is a database navigator for NXC
 
 options:
 -h, --help            show this help message and exit
 -gw, --get-workspace  get the current workspace
 -cw, --create-workspace CREATE_WORKSPACE
                       create a new workspace
 -sw, --set-workspace SET_WORKSPACE
                       set the current workspace

local_help.--help:
usage: nxcdb [-h] [-gw] [-cw CREATE_WORKSPACE] [-sw SET_WORKSPACE]

NXCDB is a database navigator for NXC

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -gw, --get-workspace  get the current workspace
  -cw, --create-workspace CREATE_WORKSPACE

  -sw, --set-workspace SET_WORKSPACE
                        set the current workspace

apt_metadata.description:
Network Execution Tool
NetExec (AKA nxc) is a network service exploitation tool that helps automate
assessing the security of large networks.
.
NetExec is the continuation of CrackMapExec, which was maintained
by mpgn over the years, but discontinued upon mpgn's retirement.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
